lede: A look at August 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alysa Liu and Kateryna Volodko, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Muhammad Yunus
events.desc: Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus takes oath as Chief Adviser to form an interim government in Bangladesh.

events.title: EuroCity
events.desc: A EuroCity express train en route from Kraków, Poland to Prague, Czech Republic strikes a part of a motorway bridge that had fallen onto the railroad track near Studénka railway station in the Czech Republic and derails, killing eight people and injuring 64 others.

events.title: Mazar-i-Sharif
events.desc: Iranian consulate in Mazar-i-Sharif, Afghanistan is raided by Taliban leading to the deaths of ten Iranian diplomats and a journalist.

events.title: Warsaw radio mast
events.desc: The Warsaw radio mast, then the tallest construction ever built, collapses.

events.title: 8888 Uprising
events.desc: The 8888 Uprising begins in Rangoon (Yangon), Burma (Myanmar). Led by students, hundreds of thousands join in nationwide protests against the one-party regime. On September 18, the demonstrations end in a military crackdown, killing thousands.

events.title: History of Wrigley Field
events.desc: The first night baseball game in the history of Chicago's Wrigley Field (game was rained out in the fourth inning).

events.title: Zebra crossing
events.desc: At a zebra crossing in London, photographer Iain Macmillan takes the iconic photo that becomes the cover image of the Beatles' album Abbey Road.

events.title: Marcinelle mining disaster
events.desc: Marcinelle mining disaster in Belgium. 262 coal miners, including a substantial number of Italian migrant workers, were killed in one of the largest mining accidents in Belgian history.

events.title: LZ 127 Graf Zeppelin
events.desc: The German airship Graf Zeppelin begins a round-the-world flight.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Following his defeat in the Battle of Gettysburg, General Robert E. Lee sends a letter of resignation to Confederate President Jefferson Davis (which is refused upon receipt).

births.name: Rudi Gernreich
births.line: Austrian-American fashion designer, created the Monokini (died 1985)

births.name: Bob Smith (doctor)
births.line: American physician and surgeon, co-founded Alcoholics Anonymous (died 1950)

deaths.text: Issa Hayatou, Cameroonian basketball player and football executive
Red Wilson (baseball), American football and baseball player
John H. Johnson, American publisher, founded the Johnson Publishing Company
Wilbert Robinson, American baseball player, coach, and manager
Carl Heinrich Graun, German tenor and composer

observances.text: Christian Feast Day: Dominic de Guzmán, founder of the Dominican Order.
